etymology.description: semantic 示 (shì, "altar") + phonetic 爾 (ěr, "you") -> personal pronoun
etymology.interpretation: The character 祢(nǐ, "you") combines the semantic component 示(shì, "altar"), suggesting a spiritual or revered aspect, with the phonetic component 爾(ěr, "you"), which provides the pronunciation. This compound illustrates how personal pronouns can be linked with spiritual or formal contexts in ancient usage.
